questions.question: Which of the following is NOT classified as a simple machine?
questions.explanation: An electric motor is a complex machine made of multiple simple machines working together. The six simple machines are lever, pulley, wheel and axle, inclined plane, screw, and wedge.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a simple machine?
options: To multiply force or change its direction
To increase the weight of an object
questions.explanation: Simple machines make work easier by multiplying the applied force or changing its direction. They do not create energy or eliminate friction.

questions.question: The Velocity Ratio (VR) of a machine is defined as:
options: Effort distance divided by load distance
Load distance divided by effort distance
questions.explanation: $VR = \text{distance moved by effort} / \text{distance moved by load}$. It is a geometric ratio based on the design of the machine.

questions.question: A machine allows a $600\text{ N}$ load to be lifted using an effort of $150\text{ N}$. What is the Mechanical Advantage (MA)?
questions.explanation: $MA = \text{Load} / \text{Effort} = 600 / 150 = 4.0$. The machine multiplies the effort force by 4.

questions.question: A worker uses a $5\text{ m}$ ramp to push a load to a height of $1.25\text{ m}$. What is the Velocity Ratio of the ramp?
A pulley system lifts a $900\text{ N}$ load when the user pulls with $300\text{ N}$ of effort. What is the Mechanical Advantage (MA)?
The efficiency of a machine is calculated using:
questions.explanation: Efficiency $= (MA / VR) \times 100\%$. It compares the actual mechanical advantage to the ideal velocity ratio.

questions.question: A machine has an MA of $5$ and a VR of $8$. What is its efficiency?
In real machines, VR is usually greater than MA. This is because:
options: Some effort is lost to friction
The load is always heavier than the effort
VR is measured in different units
questions.explanation: Friction causes some of the input energy to be converted to heat, so the actual force output (MA) is less than the ideal (VR). Efficiency is always less than 100%.

questions.question: A knife is an example of which type of simple machine?
questions.explanation: A knife is a wedge — it consists of two inclined planes joined together to split or cut materials apart.

questions.question: A pair of scissors consists of two levers working together. To which class of lever do they belong?
options: First class (fulcrum between load and effort)
Second class (load between fulcrum and effort)
Third class (effort between fulcrum and load)
questions.explanation: In scissors, the fulcrum (pivot) is between the effort (handles) and the load (blades), making them first-class levers.

questions.question: In a pulley system with 4 supporting rope segments, what is the ideal Velocity Ratio?
questions.explanation: For a pulley system, the ideal VR equals the number of rope segments supporting the load. With 4 supporting segments, VR = 4.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered the fundamentals of simple machines!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review the difference between VR and MA, and how to calculate efficiency.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on identifying machine types and using the VR and MA formulas.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: six types of simple machines, VR = effort distance / load distance, MA = load / effort.
